title: Highstreet World
description: A commerce-centered metaverse blending MMO gameplay, NFT fashion, and real-world brand integrations.
about:
Highstreet World is a phygital metaverse where fashion brands, gameplay, and e-commerce converge. Players explore brand worlds, earn and craft NFT cosmetics, and engage in an MMORPG backbone that gives real utility to in-game assets.

The platform runs cross-surface across web and VR, with a persistent multiplayer world that grows through brand land sales and live content drops. My role covered core gameplay systems, real-time multiplayer features, and tooling to support rapid content iteration from the live-ops team.
features: Cross-surface play — web and VR in the same world
MMORPG backbone with quest and encounter systems
Real-time co-op sessions with role synergies
Brand world integration with e-commerce storefronts

myWork.title: Gameplay Systems Architecture
myWork.description: Designed the modular quest, encounter, and progression loop architecture. Established clear ownership boundaries between systems so live-ops could push content without engineering bottlenecks.

myWork.title: Real-Time Multiplayer Features
myWork.description: Implemented networked gameplay features using Netcode for GameObjects — synced state, predictive movement, and graceful handling of network variance to keep sessions stable.

myWork.title: Live Content Pipeline
myWork.description: Built Addressables-based content streaming so brand world updates and seasonal drops could ship without full game builds, cutting release turnaround significantly.

title: Nutrien HGV Driver Training
description: 7 bespoke interactive scenarios training Nutrien's HGV drivers on rare but deadly accident situations.
about:
Nutrien's experienced heavy-goods and hazardous-materials drivers faced uncommon accidents with no existing training content. We built a tailored PC simulation with 7 scenarios — each recreating a specific accident type identified by Nutrien's safety team.

Every scenario ships three modes: an instructional phase with guided hints, a free-practice phase, and a scored assessment mode that integrates directly with Nutrien's existing LMS. The result is a measurable, repeatable training tool that fits their existing compliance infrastructure.
features: Realistic HGV cab and site environments

myWork.title: Scenario System Architecture
myWork.description: Designed a data-driven scenario framework so all 7 accident types could share one runtime while keeping scenario content independently authored and swappable.

myWork.title: LMS Integration
myWork.description: Implemented SCORM communication layer bridging Unity assessment scores to Nutrien's LMS, including completion state, pass/fail thresholds, and per-question breakdowns.

title: Autoliv Explore 3D
description: An AR app that lets Autoliv's global sales teams demo life-saving automotive safety products in 3D on any surface.
about:
Autoliv manufactures airbags, seatbelts, and active safety systems used across the automotive industry. Their sales and marketing teams needed a way to demo physical products interactively — without shipping hardware to every meeting.

Explore 3D uses AR marker and surface detection to place photo-real 3D product models anywhere. Reps can explode components, highlight mechanisms, and walk customers through safety certification data — all from an iPad. The app also serves as a training tool for plant operators learning product internals.
features: Sales presentation mode with guided flow

myWork.title: AR Interaction System
myWork.description: Built the AR placement, anchoring, and surface-tracking pipeline. Handled edge cases for poor lighting, marker occlusion, and device capability differences across iPhone and iPad targets.

myWork.title: Explode & Annotation Features
myWork.description: Implemented the animated component explode system and the data overlay layer, both driven from a structured product JSON so content authors could update without code changes.

title: Scriptable System
description: A Unity Editor extension that makes managing ScriptableObject variables and events fast and organized.
about:
Scriptable System was born from frustration with the boilerplate required every time you want a typed ScriptableObject variable or event in Unity. The package eliminates that friction — you name a type and the tool generates the full class, registers it, and wires up listeners in one click.

Shipped to the Unity Asset Store, it targets any Unity developer who uses the ScriptableObject event architecture pattern and wants to spend time on gameplay, not glue code.
features: Generate typed ScriptableVariable classes in seconds
Generate typed ScriptableEvent classes in seconds
One-click event listener wiring in MonoBehaviours
Pre/post response hooks on raised events
Quick-find where each asset is referenced
Configurable output path and naming conventions

myWork.title: Code Generation Engine
myWork.description: Built the reflection-based code generator that produces correctly typed ScriptableVariable and ScriptableEvent classes with proper Unity serialization attributes and editor drawers.

myWork.title: Editor UX & Workflow
myWork.description: Designed the Editor window flow, quick-find reference scanner, and auto-wiring system so the entire workflow from idea to working event listener takes under 30 seconds.

title: 10 Tawla
description: Competitive online Backgammon with ranked matchmaking, social lobbies, and smart AI — built for iOS and Android.
about:
10 Tawla modernizes the classic Arabic board game Backgammon (Tawla) for mobile. It supports two rule variants — Moultezim (Tawla 31) and Mahbuse (Plakoto) — with real-time online play, ELO-based ranking, and social features.

The multiplayer backend runs on Nakama Server with Firebase for auth and analytics. Matches are tight-loop turn-based with real-time state sync, reconnect handling, and anti-cheat checks. The AI opponent is tuned per difficulty to match the player's rank rather than play optimally, keeping casual sessions fun.
features: Ranked ELO matchmaking with skill-based pairing
Host and join private matches with friends
Smart AI difficulty matched to your ELO
7 life-like board and piece aesthetic sets

myWork.title: Real-Time Match Flow
myWork.description: Built the full turn lifecycle — dice roll sync, move validation, reconnect recovery, and timeout handling — on top of Nakama's match handler with Lua server logic.

myWork.title: Ranking & Progression System
myWork.description: Implemented ELO calculation, leaderboard writes, and anti-abuse checks on the server side, with client-side optimistic updates for snappy UX.

myWork.title: AI Opponent
myWork.description: Tuned the AI decision heuristics to different difficulty bands. The hardest mode plays strong but makes occasional mistakes, keeping the game fun rather than frustrating.

title: Sky Striders
description: A movement-first online multiplayer game where mastering aerial traversal is the core skill expression.
about:
Sky Striders is built around the idea that movement itself should be the primary gameplay loop. Players zip, glide, and vault across vertical arenas — each character class has different movement stats that change how you approach every encounter and mode.

The game features several modes that challenge players to express that movement skill under pressure: objective races, elimination, and zone control. Multiplayer runs on a custom relay setup, with server-authoritative hit detection and client-side prediction to keep the game responsive at speed.
features: Fluid ground and aerial movement mechanics
Character classes with unique movement profiles

myWork.title: Core Movement System
myWork.description: Implemented the full movement controller — acceleration curves, air control, wall interaction, and momentum preservation — with extensive tuning to achieve a snappy but readable feel.

myWork.title: Multiplayer Architecture
myWork.description: Set up Mirror-based server-authoritative networking with client-side prediction and reconciliation, ensuring the movement system felt responsive even at 150ms+ latency.

title: That Just Happened
description: A serious VR game where players prevent deadly blue-collar workplace accidents by giving co-workers the right safety gear.
about:
That Just Happened puts you inside common industrial accident scenarios — a co-worker is about to make a fatal mistake and you have seconds to grab the right safety equipment and intervene.

Built in Unreal Engine for PC VR, the game uses physical VR interactions: you reach, grab, and hand equipment to workers in real time. The scenarios are drawn from real workplace accident data, making this both engaging and genuinely educational for safety training programs.
features: Scenarios based on real accident data

myWork.title: VR Interaction Framework
myWork.description: Built the physical grab, carry, and hand-off system with haptic feedback, ensuring interactions felt natural across different hand sizes and controllers.

myWork.title: Scenario & Trigger System
myWork.description: Designed the timed scenario state machine — worker behaviour, accident trigger windows, correct equipment validation, and outcome branching — using Unreal Blueprints.

title: Steam Blast
description: A multiplayer VR fighting game where players fly, attack, and capture city devices — built as an ITI graduation project.
about:
Steam Blast is a competitive multiplayer VR game set in a steampunk city. Players fly freely through the arena, exchange ranged and melee attacks, and race to capture control points that determine the city's fate.

Built in Unreal Engine as the capstone project for the ITI Game Development diploma. The project covered full VR physics flight, networked multiplayer, and a capture-the-objective game mode from scratch — a significant technical scope for a student project.
features: Melee and ranged combat in zero-G
Custom VR locomotion with no motion sickness design

myWork.title: VR Flight Mechanics
myWork.description: Designed and implemented the 3D flight system with momentum, drag, and comfort-mode options. Iterated extensively to minimize discomfort while preserving the sense of speed.

myWork.title: Multiplayer Combat Loop
myWork.description: Built the networked combat — projectile replication, hit detection, respawn flow, and objective score tracking — over Photon PUN.

title: Killing Beat
description: A rhythm mobile game where you dodge obstacles perfectly in sync with the beat.
about:
Killing Beat is a hyper-casual rhythm game designed around one tight loop: obstacles come at you timed to the music, and your job is to dodge them by reading the beat before the visual cue arrives.

The game's design challenge was making the music-obstacle sync feel fair and satisfying — not punishing. Custom audio analysis tools were built to map beat events to obstacle spawn timings, allowing designers to author levels without manual frame-counting.

myWork.title: Audio-to-Obstacle Sync System
myWork.description: Built a custom DSP pipeline that analyses audio frequency peaks and emits timed gameplay events, driving obstacle spawns without manual timeline authoring.

myWork.title: Difficulty Progression
myWork.description: Designed the difficulty scaling system that increases obstacle density, speed, and pattern complexity based on session performance to maintain flow state.

title: SymSam
description: Play as a samurai slicing geometric shapes into perfect halves — precision is everything.
about:
SymSam is a hyper-casual mobile game built around a single satisfying mechanic: drawing a symmetrical cut through increasingly complex shapes. The samurai character gives the cutting gesture personality, and the geometric shapes escalate in complexity as the player progresses.

The core engineering challenge was a robust real-time polygon slicing system that could handle arbitrary convex and concave shapes, compute symmetry error, and provide snappy visual feedback on each cut.

myWork.title: Polygon Slice Engine
myWork.description: Implemented the real-time polygon slicing system using computational geometry — handling convex/concave shape decomposition, UV remapping on cut faces, and physics re-initialisation of resulting pieces.

myWork.title: Symmetry Scoring
myWork.description: Built the symmetry measurement algorithm that computes the axis of the player's cut and scores deviation from perfect bilateral symmetry, with tiered feedback (Perfect / Good / Miss).

title: Bomb Party
description: 4-player PVP where you throw bombs directly or through portals until one player survives — built in 48 hours for Global Game Jam 2018.
about:
Bomb Party was built for Global Game Jam 2018 on the theme of Transmission. Four players compete on a shared screen, throwing bombs that bounce off walls or travel through portals to reach opponents.

The game jam constraint forced rapid prototyping: portal mechanics, physics-based bomb trajectories, and local split-input were all built and iterated within 48 hours. The result is a chaotic party game that's immediately readable and genuinely fun.
features: Built in 48 hours — Global Game Jam 2018

myWork.title: Portal & Bomb Mechanics
myWork.description: Designed and built the portal entry/exit system and bomb physics in Unity 2D — including trajectory prediction, wall bounce, portal direction mapping, and explosion radius.

myWork.title: Local Multiplayer Input
myWork.description: Set up the split-controller input layer supporting up to 4 simultaneous gamepads with correct input isolation and player assignment.

title: Skull Switch
description: Protect your skulls by toggling their lane visibility before incoming obstacles destroy them.
about:
Skull Switch is a reflex-driven hyper-casual game with a simple but escalating challenge: skulls appear in lanes, obstacles race toward them, and you tap to toggle a skull's visibility — making it pass through obstacles harmlessly. Miss the window and the skull is gone.

The game layers complexity by increasing lane count, obstacle speed, and simultaneous threat count, while keeping the core gesture (tap to toggle) constant. The main design challenge was calibrating the timing windows to feel fair at high difficulty.
features: Visual clarity design for split-second reads

myWork.title: Lane & Visibility System
myWork.description: Built the lane management and visibility toggle system with correct collision masking so toggled skulls are truly transparent to obstacles without physics edge cases.

myWork.title: Difficulty Curve Tuning
myWork.description: Designed the parameterised difficulty ramp — obstacle speed, simultaneous lane threat count, and timing windows — iterating on playtests to land the difficulty curve in the flow-state sweet spot.
